howItWorks.intro: This australia payg tax calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Without tax-free threshold (second job): 30% from first dollar up to $135,000
Super Guarantee: 11.5% of ordinary time earnings (2024-25)
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What is PAYG withholding?
faq.answer: Pay As You Go (PAYG) withholding is the system where your employer deducts income tax from your wages each pay period and sends it to the ATO. The amount withheld is based on your declared tax-free threshold status and income level.

faq.question: Should I claim the tax-free threshold?
faq.answer: Claim the tax-free threshold at your main job only. If you have multiple jobs, only one employer should apply the threshold. Not claiming it at a second job avoids a large tax debt at tax time.

faq.question: How much super does my employer pay?
faq.answer: Employers must pay Superannuation Guarantee (SG) of 11.5% (2024-25) on top of your ordinary time earnings, rising to 12% from July 2025. Super is generally paid on top of your salary, not deducted from it.

faq.question: Will I get a tax refund?
faq.answer: You may receive a refund if your employer withheld more tax than needed, or if you have deductible expenses (work-related, self-education, donations). Lodge your tax return after 30 June to find out your actual tax position.
